subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Paper Boats
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Day by day, a young child floats paper boats one by one down the running stream. The water is clear and carries the boats away quickly. The child watches them with great interest as they sail into the distance.
questions: How often does the child float the boats?
Where are the boats being floated?
What is the condition of the water?
How do the boats move?
How does the child feel while watching the boats?

passages.paragraph: On each paper boat, the child writes their name in big black letters. They also write the name of the village where they live. The child hopes that someone in a strange land will find the boats and know who sent them.
questions: What does the child write on the boats?
What color are the letters?
What other piece of information is written on the boats?
Who does the child hope will find the boats?
Where is the 'strange land' located in the child's imagination?

passages.paragraph: The child loads the boats with 'Shiuli' flowers gathered from the garden. These small, orange-centered flowers are very delicate. The child hopes that these blooms of the dawn will be carried safely to land in the night.
questions: What kind of flowers are put in the boats?
Where are the flowers gathered from?
How are the flowers described?
What time of day are the flowers called 'blooms' of?
When does the child hope the flowers will reach land?

passages.paragraph: When the child looks up at the sky, they see little clouds setting their white bulging sails. It looks like another child in the sky is playing a similar game. The clouds seem to be racing with the paper boats below.
questions: Where does the child look?
What are the clouds compared to?
What color are the sails of the clouds?
Who does the child imagine is playing in the sky?
What are the clouds doing with the paper boats?

passages.paragraph: Sometimes, the wind is strong and pushes the boats toward the muddy banks. The child uses a long stick to push them back into the middle of the stream. It is important that the boats keep moving forward.
questions: What happens when the wind is strong?
Where are the boats pushed toward?
What tool does the child use?
Where does the child push the boats back to?
What is important for the boats to do?

passages.paragraph: When night comes, the child buries their face in their arms and dreams. In the dream, the paper boats are still floating out under the midnight stars. The fairies of sleep are sailing in them.
questions: What does the child do when night comes?
What is the child doing in the dream?
What is in the sky in the dream?
Who is sailing in the boats in the dream?
Are the boats still floating in the dream?

passages.paragraph: The fairies of sleep carry baskets full of dreams in the paper boats. These dreams are soft and sweet. The boats glide smoothly over the dark water as the world sleeps in silence.
questions: What are the fairies carrying?
Where are the baskets kept?
How are the dreams described?
How do the boats move over the water?
What is the world doing while the boats glide?

passages.paragraph: Every morning, the child wakes up and finds that some boats have disappeared. They might have reached a new place or sunk in the deep water. The child simply smiles and starts making new ones with fresh paper.
questions: What does the child find in the morning?
What are two things that might have happened to the boats?
Does the child cry when the boats are gone?
What does the child do after waking up?
What kind of paper is used for the new boats?

passages.paragraph: Making paper boats is a simple joy. It requires only a square piece of paper and a little bit of folding. The child enjoys the sound of the paper crinkling as the edges are tucked in to make a hull.
questions: Is making paper boats difficult?
What shape of paper is needed?
What action is needed to make the boat?
What sound does the paper make?
What part of the boat is made by tucking in the edges?

passages.paragraph: The story of paper boats teaches us about hope and imagination. Even a small toy can connect us to people far away. Like the child, we should always send our best wishes out into the world.
questions: What two things does the story teach us about?
What can connect us to people far away?
Is the toy described as large or small?
What should we send out into the world?
Who should we be like according to the text?
